audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for divine word/vision, fear-not formula, shield/reward, Abram's trust, reckoned righteousness, covenant cutting, deep sleep/dread, exile prophecy, fire-pot/torch theophany, and land-boundary promise. Final source-critical review may refine difficult idioms.

verses.restored: After these things, the word of the Eternal came to Abram in a vision, saying, Do not fear, Abram. I am your shield; your reward is very great.

familiar.text: After these things the word of the LORD came unto Abram in a vision, saying, Fear not, Abram...

notes.title: Word in a vision
notes.body: The divine word comes in a vision, linking speech and seeing.

notes.title: Shield and reward
notes.body: After Abram refuses Sodom’s wealth, the Eternal names himself as shield and great reward.

verses.restored: Abram said, Lord Eternal, what will you give me, since I go childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?

familiar.text: And Abram said, Lord GOD, what wilt thou give me, seeing I go childless...

notes.title: Lord Eternal
notes.body: Adonai YHWH is rendered Lord Eternal. Abram responds to promise with the unresolved wound of childlessness.

verses.restored: Abram said, Look, you have given me no seed, and behold, a son of my house will inherit from me.

familiar.text: And Abram said, Behold, to me thou hast given no seed: and, lo, one born in my house is mine heir.

notes.title: No seed
notes.body: The seed promise is still unresolved, and Abram names that tension directly.

verses.restored: And behold, the word of the Eternal came to him, saying, This one shall not inherit from you, but one who comes out from your own body shall inherit from you.

familiar.text: And, behold, the word of the LORD came unto him, saying, This shall not be thine heir...

notes.title: From your own body
notes.body: Literally from your inward parts. The heir will come from Abram himself.

verses.restored: He brought him outside and said, Look now toward the heavens, and count the stars, if you are able to count them. And he said to him, So shall your seed be.

familiar.text: And he brought him forth abroad, and said, Look now toward heaven, and tell the stars...

notes.title: Stars as seed image
notes.body: The uncountable stars become the image of Abram’s promised seed.

verses.restored: And he trusted in the Eternal, and he reckoned it to him as righteousness.

familiar.text: And he believed in the LORD; and he counted it to him for righteousness.

notes.title: Trusted/believed
notes.body: He'emin means trusted, believed, or put faith in. Trust keeps the relational quality visible.

notes.title: Reckoned as righteousness
notes.body: Chashav...tsedaqah means counted, reckoned, or considered as righteousness. This becomes a major later theological verse.

verses.restored: He said to him, I am the Eternal who brought you out from Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess it.

familiar.text: And he said unto him, I am the LORD that brought thee out of Ur of the Chaldees...

notes.title: Brought you out
notes.body: The promise is grounded in divine action already begun: Abram was brought out to receive land.

verses.restored: He said, Lord Eternal, how shall I know that I shall possess it?

familiar.text: And he said, Lord GOD, whereby shall I know that I shall inherit it?

notes.title: How shall I know?
notes.body: Abram’s trust does not erase his request for confirmation concerning land.

verses.restored: He said to him, Bring me a three-year-old heifer, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.

familiar.text: And he said unto him, Take me an heifer of three years old...

notes.title: Covenant animals
notes.body: The animals prepare for the covenant-cutting ritual that follows.

verses.restored: He brought all these to him, cut them in two, and placed each half opposite the other; but the birds he did not cut.

familiar.text: And he took unto him all these, and divided them in the midst...

notes.title: Cut covenant setting
notes.body: Cutting animals and passing between pieces is covenant-ritual background. The Hebrew idiom for making covenant is often cut a covenant.

verses.restored: Birds of prey came down upon the carcasses, and Abram drove them away.

familiar.text: And when the fowls came down upon the carcases, Abram drove them away.

verses.restored: As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell on Abram, and behold, a terror, a great darkness, fell upon him.

familiar.text: And when the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram; and, lo, an horror of great darkness fell upon him.

notes.title: Deep sleep
notes.body: Tardemah recalls the deep sleep of Adam in Genesis 2. Here it accompanies covenant revelation and dread.

verses.restored: He said to Abram, Know surely that your seed will be a sojourner in a land not theirs. They will serve them, and they will afflict them four hundred years.

familiar.text: And he said unto Abram, Know of a surety that thy seed shall be a stranger in a land that is not theirs...

notes.title: Know surely
notes.body: The Hebrew intensifies knowing: Abram receives hard knowledge about future exile and affliction.

notes.title: Serve and afflict
notes.body: The prophecy anticipates Israel’s later bondage.

verses.restored: But the nation whom they serve I will judge, and afterward they shall go out with great possessions.

familiar.text: And also that nation, whom they shall serve, will I judge: and afterward shall they come out with great substance.

notes.title: Go out with possessions
notes.body: The prophecy anticipates Exodus: judgment on the oppressor and departure with possessions.

verses.restored: And you shall go to your fathers in peace. You shall be buried at a good old age.

familiar.text: And thou shalt go to thy fathers in peace; thou shalt be buried in a good old age.

verses.restored: In the fourth generation they shall return here, for the iniquity of the Amorite is not yet complete.

familiar.text: But in the fourth generation they shall come hither again: for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet full.

notes.title: Not yet complete
notes.body: The land promise is tied to moral timing; the Amorite iniquity is not yet full.

verses.restored: When the sun had gone down and deep darkness came, behold, a smoking fire-pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, that, when the sun went down, and it was dark... a smoking furnace, and a burning lamp...

notes.title: Fire theophany
notes.body: The smoking fire-pot and flaming torch represent divine presence passing between the covenant pieces.

verses.restored: On that day the Eternal cut a covenant with Abram, saying, To your seed I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates:

familiar.text: In the same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land...

notes.title: Cut a covenant
notes.body: The Hebrew idiom is cut a covenant, matching the cut animals in the ritual.

verses.restored: the Kenite, the Kenizzite, the Kadmonite,

familiar.text: The Kenites, and the Kenizzites, and the Kadmonites,

verses.restored: the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Rephaim,

familiar.text: And the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Rephaims,

verses.restored: the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Girgashite, and the Jebusite.

familiar.text: And the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
